name: Noski Deadpan 2-Hander
description: Deadpan-philosophical TikTok 2-hander in the @americanbaron register: two people seated side-by-side in a sunlit living room, heads tipped back on the couch, eyes up to the ceiling, exchanging quiet half-rhetorical questions in almost-whispered English. Photoreal Sony A7 IV / Sigma-prime register with Kodak Portra 400 grain. ~32 micro-scenes averaging 2.2s each, 73-75s total, lofi cafe music bed at -22 LUFS under the dialog. Reference-required when imitating a real creator's persona — the @handle drives voice profile, posture vocabulary, and the deadpan delivery cadence.

stackSummary.keyframes: Master portraits via openai/gpt-5.4-image-2 (premium photoreal skin at single-portrait scale). Location-master-plate + every scene anchor via google/gemini-3-pro-image-preview with multi-ref: --ref location-master-plate.png + --ref master-character.png. Multi-ref is load-bearing: skipping the location plate produces a different physical couch per call.
stackSummary.i2v: kwaivgi/kling-v3.0-pro --audio for every dialog clip. 3-5s clips, static camera quadruple-banned, voice profile block + 'Same voice as earlier scenes.' anchor for cross-clip voice consistency. Music explicitly banned in every Kling prompt (sevenfold negative). Two-speaker finale can be one merged 5s top-down clip with timed dialog segments.
stackSummary.voiceover: Native to Kling --audio for English target audience. NO separate ElevenLabs VO pass. If target is non-English (RU/UA/etc) — Kling --audio is unsafe (accent slip, age drift); fall back to ElevenLabs eleven_multilingual_v2 post-mix and disable Kling --audio.
stackSummary.captions: Optional. The format works without on-screen text; if used, MinimalCaptions at the hook beat only. Full-video Hormozi captions push it into 'ad' pattern-recognition.
stackSummary.music: ElevenLabs Music — single lofi cafe instrumental bed at 65 BPM, -22 LUFS, full-length (~110s for a 73s video with 1.5s fade in / 2s fade out). Music sits at -18dB under VO with optional sidechain ducking. Never name artists in the prompt (ToS rejection).
stackSummary.render: ffmpeg-based hard-cut concat (no Remotion needed for the deadpan register — hard cuts, no animated overlays). Per-clip pre-process: ffprobe real duration, Scribe word timestamps for speech-aware trim, 50ms audio fade in/out before concat to kill the AAC-boundary click bug.

constants.structure: 0-3s establishing wide (location-master-plate as scene-01) → 3-65s alternating dialog micro-scenes (avg 2.2s) → 65-75s top-down two-speaker finale + held silent beat

variationAxes: topic (existential / mundane / temporal / object-vs-person / loss / memory)
cast gender pair (M+W / W+W / M+M / non-binary)
ethnicity / age / styling (defines master portraits; rest of vibe stays)
location (living room / sunroom / wood-floor cafe / library / porch — anything with one continuous back-cushion ridge to rest the head on)
music profile (lofi cafe 65 BPM default / acoustic indie 70 BPM / quiet ambient pad 60 BPM)
language (English with Kling --audio canonical / other languages via ElevenLabs post-mix)
doNotCopyLiterally: Cast identity, exact dialog lines, specific location (couch/wall/rug), topic of the question. Vibe reference only — the discipline is photoreal-seated-head-back-deadpan-philosophy with location-locked multi-ref, not the specific couch.
referenceNotes: Persona reference (the @handle being imitated, or user-supplied character photos) is REQUIRED. AGENTS.md hard rule #3 applies: a named real creator is a named real entity. Without a reference: refuse, or accept 'no-ref-consent' and log. The whole format is voice-profile + posture + location-discipline driven — improvising the cast from text alone loses the deadpan register within one clip.

slots.description: First speaker. Visual descriptor + ethnicity + age band + wardrobe + identity quirks (asymmetry, mole, freckles). Source example: 'Black man, late 20s, shoulder-length thin natural locs, thin gold wire-rim eyeglasses, chocolate-brown merino turtleneck, small silver pinky ring.'

Speaker A 7-tag voice profile block: gender, age, voice-tone (baritone/contralto), voice-texture (gravel/breathy), delivery register (deadpan-philosophical), accent (neutral American), volume (quiet/almost-whispered). Example: 'Speaker A: Black man, late 20s, soft warm baritone voice with slight gravel, deadpan philosophical delivery, neutral American accent, quiet contemplative volume, almost-whispered register.'

Second speaker. Same descriptor shape as character_a. Source example: 'Freckled red-haired young woman, mid-20s, lavender-tipped copper hair, slight overbite, cream-and-mustard cable-knit cardigan over white t-shirt, no makeup, dense freckles across cheeks/nose/eyelids.'

Speaker B 7-tag voice profile block. Example: 'Speaker B: young pale white woman, mid-20s, soft contralto voice, slight breathy quality, deadpan philosophical delivery, neutral American accent, slight overbite, quiet contemplative volume, almost-whispered register.'

Wide front establishing shot of the room with BOTH characters seated head-back on the couch. Locks couch geometry, rug, wall color, lamp, props, lighting direction. This image is anchor #1 and is passed as --ref on every subsequent scene anchor. Source example: 'low wide cream boucle modular three-seat couch with one continuous low back-cushion ridge, dusty-pink matte wall, vintage Persian rug in faded reds and rust-blues, dark walnut mid-century sideboard with handmade matte stoneware ceramic vessels, opal-glass globe ceiling pendant, soft north-window daylight from camera-left.'
Target audience language. EN is canonical (Kling --audio path). Anything else → Kling --audio off, post-mix ElevenLabs eleven_multilingual_v2.

One-line philosophical seed for the half-rhetorical Q&A. Source example: 'Socks or people — what gets lost more often?' Other variants: 'Doors or memories — which close more silently?', 'Names or birthdays — which are forgotten first?'
The @handle being imitated. Example: '@americanbaron "Do more things exist or not exist?"'. Used as research reference; never substituted into generation prompts directly.

master_character_a_portrait.note: Source-project canonical example. Replace with the new project's Speaker A master portrait.

master_character_b_portrait.note: Source-project canonical example. Replace with the new project's Speaker B master portrait.

location_master_plate.note: Source-project canonical example. Anchor #1 — locks couch + room. Replace with the new project's wide establishing image.

top_down_tight.note: Source-project finale anchor — top-down view of both heads on the cushion ridge. Useful reference for the merged two-speaker finale (rule 9 / pattern 6 in TEMPLATE.md).

music_bed.note: Lofi cafe instrumental, 65 BPM, -22 LUFS, instrumental, ~110s. Drop-in usable for any 60-90s deadpan-philosophical piece. Mix at -18dB under VO.
